meta.title: Testing Strategy - Quality Gates
meta.excerpt: A comprehensive strategy for unit, integration, and end-to-end testing across the platform.

seo.metaDescription: Build confidence with a layered testing strategy for modern applications.

blocks.content: Quality engineering relies on balancing test speed, reliability, and coverage across multiple test layers.

blocks.title: Execution Discipline
blocks.content: Keep E2E tests focused on high-value flows to avoid slow and brittle pipelines.
Unit tests form the foundation of the testing strategy, providing fast feedback on individual functions and components. We use Vitest for its speed and compatibility with Next.js.

features.title: Fast Execution
features.description: Run hundreds of tests in seconds with Vitest

features.title: Isolated Tests
features.description: Mock external dependencies for true unit isolation

features.title: Component Testing
features.description: Test React components with React Testing Library

features.title: Business Logic
features.description: Test utility functions and data transformations

blocks.title: Unit Test Example

items: Test file location: co-located with source files (*.test.ts)
Coverage target: 70% minimum for critical paths
Mock external dependencies (API calls, third-party libraries)
Focus on behavior, not implementation details
Use descriptive test names that explain expected behavior
Group related tests with describe blocks

blocks.title: Testing Best Practice
blocks.content: Write tests that describe what the code should do, not how it does it. This makes tests resilient to refactoring.
Integration tests verify that multiple units work together correctly. They test API routes, data flows, and component interactions without mocking internal dependencies.

features.title: API Routes
features.description: Test Next.js API routes and server actions

blocks.title: Integration Test Example

items: Test file location: __tests__/integration-test/
Coverage target: 20% of total tests
Use test database or fixtures for data layer tests
Test actual API endpoint behavior with supertest or fetch
Verify error handling and validation logic
Test authentication and authorization middleware

blocks.content: End-to-end tests validate complete user workflows in a browser environment. We use Playwright for reliable, cross-browser E2E testing.

features.title: User Journeys
features.description: Test critical paths from user perspective

features.title: Cross-Browser
features.description: Verify functionality in Chrome, Firefox, Safari

features.title: Mobile Testing
features.description: Test responsive behavior on mobile devices

blocks.title: E2E Test Example with Playwright

items: Test file location: __tests__/e2e/
Coverage target: 10% of total tests (high-value journeys only)
Run E2E tests on staging environment before production
Use page object pattern for maintainable tests
Implement retry logic for flaky network operations
Capture screenshots and videos on test failure

blocks.title: E2E Test Performance
blocks.content: E2E tests are slow and expensive. Focus on critical user journeys only and run them on CI for main branch merges.
Proper test organization ensures tests are easy to find, maintain, and run selectively during development.

blocks.title: Test Directory Structure
blocks.code:
__tests__/
├── unit/
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── atoms/
│   │   ├── molecules/
│   │   └── organisms/
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── utils/
│   │   └── strapi/
│   └── hooks/
├── integration-test/
│   ├── api/
│   ├── repositories/
│   └── features/
├── e2e/
│   ├── user-journeys/
│   ├── critical-paths/
│   └── smoke-tests/
└── mocks/
    ├── strapi/
    ├── api/
    └── fixtures/

items: Co-locate unit tests with source files when appropriate
Group integration tests by feature or domain
Organize E2E tests by user journey or workflow
Share mocks and fixtures in __tests__/mocks/
Use consistent naming: *.test.ts for unit, *.spec.ts for integration
Tag tests with @unit, @integration, @e2e for selective runs

blocks.title: Running Tests Selectively
blocks.content: Use 'pnpm test:unit' for quick feedback during development, and 'pnpm test:all' before committing changes.

features.title: Watch Mode
features.description: Auto-run tests on file changes during development

features.title: Selective Runs
features.description: Run specific test suites or files with pattern matching
